commands.author: abnershang

target.title: fix(openai): harden Codex OAuth callback cleanup
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #81865.

This hardens the OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex OAuth flow in two places:

- token exchange and refresh now convert malformed/non-JSON successful token responses into structured OAuth failures instead of leaking a raw JSON parser exception
- the local OAuth callback server marks HTML responses as `Connection: close` and destroys accepted callback sockets during cleanup so a callback connection cannot pin the auth CLI after success

This PR was prepared with AI assistance.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts -- --run -t "completes browser callback login" --reporter verbose`
  - Passed. The named controlled callback-login test drove `loginOpenAICodex` through the real local callback server and mocked only the token endpoint.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-openai-chatgpt-oauth.test.ts`
  - Passed 2 Vitest shards: OpenAI provider OAuth facade tests and OpenAI OAuth flow tests. The OpenAI OAuth flow shard passed 14 tests.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - Passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - Passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --ignore-path .gitignore extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.ts extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts`
  - Passed.
- `git diff --check`
  - Passed.
- Dependency guard: checked the patch for dependency/package/lockfile changes; none changed.
- Codex review: `codex review --uncommitted`
  - No actionable regressions found after the follow-up proof/test changes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex OAuth should turn malformed successful token responses into structured OAuth failures, and the localhost OAuth callback path should release accepted callback sockets after the browser callback completes.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source worktree on Node `v26.0.0`, running the OpenAI plugin OAuth callback runtime from this PR head.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from repo root with a temporary source-runtime callback probe. The probe imported `extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.ts`, started `testing.startLocalOAuthServer("proof-state-81865")`, sent a keep-alive request to `/auth/callback?state=proof-state-81865&code=proof-code-81865`, called `close()`, and exited non-zero if the callback code, close header, or socket cleanup check did not match.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the source-runtime callback probe:

```text
OpenClaw OAuth callback source-runtime proof
node_version=v26.0.0
callback_host=localhost
callback_result_code=proof-code-81865
http_status=HTTP/1.1 200 OK
connection_header=Connection: close
socket_closed_after_cleanup=true
elapsed_ms=24
```

Observed result after fix: The callback delivered `proof-code-81865`, returned `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`, wrote `Connection: close`, and the accepted socket was closed during cleanup.

What was not tested: No live browser sign-in with real OpenAI credentials was performed, and no secrets were used. Full repository suites were not run.
target.author: abnershang
